the jet velocity can be assumed to remain constant. Therefore, the horizontal distance traveled by jet (x) in time (t) is equal to:

Therefore, for steady flow conditions (i.e., constant h in the head tank), the value of Cv can be determined from the x, y coordinates of the jet trajectory. A graph of x plotted against will have

a slope of 2Cv.
